title: About the Labour Cost Survey (changes to salary and wage rates) | Stats NZ
content:
## About the Labour Cost Survey (changes to salary and wage rates)
21 September 2023, 10:45am
Information about taking part in the Labour Cost Survey, such as why we carry out the survey, how we selected you, and how to complete the questionnaire.
We carry out this survey to collect information on salary and wage rates. The information collected is compared with the previous quarter to establish the comparative change or movement in salary and wage rate over time. We use this to calculate the labour cost index.
The target population for this survey is large. It includes all occupations in each industry in each sector of the New Zealand economy.
We select businesses that employ representative job positions from each sector of ownership and industry. You were selected because your business currently employs job positions which are selected to be included in the survey.
The questions included in this survey are being asked under the Data and Statistics Act 2022.
Our approach to survey compliance has more information about your legal obligation to participate in the Labour Cost Survey.
To accurately track the change in salary and wage rates, we need to survey the same participants each quarter.
Unfortunately, we can’t tell you exactly how long your business will be included in the survey sample for. This is because re-selection of the sample for any particular occupation varies according to how much change occurs within an industry, and the sector of ownership.
The survey is carried out every quarter.

## How to complete the survey
You fill in a paper or electronic (Excel) questionnaire.
- Contact us for survey help 26 May 2020
- Business survey information 31 July 2017
- Privacy, security, and confidentiality of survey data 20 May 2026
- Economy 01 January 2017 Image: money coins
